name: GPT-4o
summary: OpenAI's flagship multimodal model with real-time audio, vision, and text capabilities.

name: o3
summary: OpenAI's most powerful reasoning model, optimized for complex multi-step problems.

name: o4-mini
summary: Compact, cost-efficient reasoning model from OpenAI with strong coding ability.

name: Claude 3.7 Sonnet
summary: Anthropic's most intelligent model to date, featuring extended thinking for complex reasoning.

name: Claude 3.5 Haiku
summary: Anthropic's fastest and most compact model, optimized for speed and cost-efficiency.

name: Gemini 2.0 Flash
summary: Google's workhorse multimodal model balancing speed, capability, and cost.

name: Gemini 2.0 Flash Thinking
summary: Google's reasoning-focused variant of Flash, capable of extended chain-of-thought.

name: Grok 3
summary: xAI's flagship model trained on Colossus supercluster, with strong reasoning capabilities.

name: Llama 3.3 70B
summary: Meta's state-of-the-art open-source instruction-tuned model, competitive with much larger closed models.

name: Llama 4 Scout
summary: Meta's multimodal MoE model with 17B active parameters and 10M token context window.

name: Llama 4 Maverick
summary: Meta's high-performance multimodal MoE model with 17B active parameters and 128 experts.

name: Mistral Large 2
summary: Mistral's most powerful model with 128K context, strong code and reasoning performance.

name: Qwen2.5 72B
summary: Alibaba's flagship open-source model excelling at coding, math, and instruction following.

name: DeepSeek-R1
summary: DeepSeek's open-source reasoning model matching OpenAI o1 performance at a fraction of training cost.

name: DeepSeek-V3
summary: DeepSeek's efficient 671B MoE model with exceptional coding and reasoning capabilities.

name: Phi-4
summary: Microsoft's compact 14B model with strong math and reasoning, punching above its weight class.

name: Gemma 3 27B
summary: Google's best open-weight model, handling text and images with 128K context.
